com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXLogicDiagramPinList

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXDiagramBaseInstance, IXDiagramBaseObject, IXDiagramCompoundObject, IXDiagramObject, IXLogicDiagramObject, IXObject, IXWriteableObject

    public interface IXLogicDiagramPinList
extends IXLogicDiagramObject
    Interface to represent Logic diagram PinList(any object which contains pins) objects.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXLogicDiagramPinList> getAttachedPinListObjects()
      Get Logic diagram pinList objects which are attached to this Logic diagram pinList object This method never returns null.
      java.util.Set<IXLogicDiagramPin> getPins()
      Get Logic diagram pins which are represented by this Logic diagram pinList object.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXLogicDiagramStackedPin> getStackedPins()
      Get Logic diagram stacked pins which are represented by this Logic diagram pinList object.

    • Method Detail

      • getAttachedPinListObjects

        java.util.Set<IXLogicDiagramPinList> getAttachedPinListObjects()
        Get Logic diagram pinList objects which are attached to this Logic diagram pinList object This method never returns null. If there are no attached objects, it returns empty set.

        Example:

        The following example checks if a Logic diagram device has any attached objects 
         boolean deviceHasAtatchedObjects(IXLogicDiagramPinList xObject) {
        if (xObject.getConnectivity() instanceof IXDevice) {
                Set<IXLogicDiagramPinList> attachedObjects = xObject.getAttachedPinListObjects();
                retrun !attachedObjects.isEmpty();
         }
        return false;
 }
        Returns:
        a Set of IXLogicDiagramPinList attached to this diagram pinList object

      • getPins

        java.util.Set<IXLogicDiagramPin> getPins()
        Get Logic diagram pins which are represented by this Logic diagram pinList object. This method never returns null. If there are no pins, it returns empty set.

        Example:

        The following example checks if a Logic diagram device has at least one logic diagram pin 
                boolean deviceHasPins(IXLogicDiagramPinList xObject) {
                if (xObject.getConnectivity() instanceof IXDevice) {
                        Set<IXLogicDiagramPin> pins = xObject.getPins();
                        return !pins.isEmpty();
                }
                return false;
        }
        Returns:
        a Set of IXLogicDiagramPin represented by this diagram pinList object

      • getStackedPins

        java.util.Set<? extends IXLogicDiagramStackedPin> getStackedPins()
        Get Logic diagram stacked pins which are represented by this Logic diagram pinList object. This method never returns null. If there are no stacked pins, it returns empty set.

        Example:

        The following example checks if a Logic diagram device has at least one stacked pin 
                boolean deviceHasStackedPins(IXLogicDiagramPinList xObject) {
                if (xObject.getConnectivity() instanceof IXDevice) {
                        Set<IXLogicDiagramStackedPin> pins = xObject.getStackedPins();
                        return !pins.isEmpty();
                }
                return false;
        }
        Returns:
        a Set of IXLogicDiagramStackedPin represented by this diagram pinList object
